title: Calandrinia umbellata
intro: Calandrinia umbellata, also known as Purple Purslane, is a small, succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and edible, nutritious leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The bioactive constituents of Calandrinia umbellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Calandrinia umbellata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to address specific health concerns.
taxonomy: Calandrinia umbellata is classified within the family Montiaceae. This classification is part of the larger order Caryophyllales. The genus Calandrinia is native to the Americas, and the species umbellata is one of the many within this genus. Calandrinia umbellata is further classified into its kingdom as a plant, specifically within the kingdom Plantae. The species is also known to be native to South America.
